_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in Pre-A1/taipei/zoo dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Anna; computer plays Tom. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: Taipei · At the Zoo

npc.text: Hi, Anna. Look! We are at the zoo.

routes.meaning: Anna says she can see a big lion.
examples: Wow! I can see a big lion.
I can see a big lion.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Wow! I can see a big lion.”

routes.meaning: Anna is excited to be at the zoo.
routes.hint: You can say “We are at the zoo!” or “Wow! I can see a big lion.”

partial.message: Tell Tom what you see: “Wow! I can see a big lion.”

off_topic.message: You are at the zoo! Say “Wow! I can see a big lion.”

npc.text: Yes! So many animals. Look at that big lion!

routes.meaning: Anna says she can see a big lion.
examples: Wow! I can see a big lion.
I can see a big lion.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Wow! I can see a big lion.”

partial.message: Say what you see: “Wow! I can see a big lion.”

off_topic.message: Look at the lion! Say “Wow! I can see a big lion.”

npc.text: I like the lion. It is big.

routes.meaning: Anna says she can see a panda too.
examples: Look! I can see a panda too.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Look! I can see a panda too.”

examples: Me too, I like the lion.
routes.hint: You can say “I like the lion too.” or “Look! I can see a panda too.”

partial.message: Tell Tom about another animal: “Look! I can see a panda too.”

off_topic.message: Look around the zoo. Say “Look! I can see a panda too.”

npc.text: The panda is black and white.

routes.meaning: Anna says she likes pandas and they are cute.
examples: I like pandas. They are cute.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I like pandas. They are cute.”

partial.message: Tell Tom you like pandas: “I like pandas. They are cute.”

off_topic.message: The panda is black and white! Say “I like pandas. They are cute.”

learning_objective: Say what the monkey can do

routes.meaning: Anna says yes and the monkey can jump.
examples: I see the monkey. It can jump!
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Yes! The monkey can jump.”

routes.meaning: Anna cannot find the monkey and asks where.
routes.hint: You can say “Where is the monkey?” or “Yes! The monkey can jump.”

partial.message: Tell Tom about the monkey: “Yes! The monkey can jump.”

off_topic.message: Tom asked about the monkey. Say “Yes! The monkey can jump.”

npc.text: There! Look up. The monkey can jump.

learning_objective: Say what the monkey can do

examples: I see it. It can jump!
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Yes! The monkey can jump.”

partial.message: Look up! Say “Yes! The monkey can jump.”

off_topic.message: There is the monkey. Say “Yes! The monkey can jump.”

npc.text: This zoo is fun. I am happy.

routes.meaning: Anna is happy too and wants to take the MRT next.
examples: Me too! Now let us take the MRT.
Me too! Let us take the MRT.
I am happy too. Let us take the MRT.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Me too! Now let us take the MRT.”

partial.message: Say what to do next: “Me too! Now let us take the MRT.”

off_topic.message: The zoo was fun! Say “Me too! Now let us take the MRT.”

npc.text: Great! The zoo was so much fun. Let us take the MRT. Bye-bye!
